2031                                          {
2032   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
2033 
2034   // Validate client pointer immediately before any access.
2035   // This prevents crashes if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct
2036   // while the thread was still starting at RtlUserThreadStart.
2037   if (!client) {
2038     log_error("Invalid client info in send thread (NULL pointer)");
2039     return NULL;
2040   }
2041 
2042   // Check if client_id is empty (client struct has been zeroed by remove_client)
2043   // This must be checked BEFORE accessing any client fields
2044   if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
2045     log_debug_every(100 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2046                     "Send thread: client_id is empty, client struct may have been zeroed, exiting");
2047     return NULL;
2048   }
2049 
2050   // Additional validation: check socket OR transport is valid
2051   // For TCP clients: socket is valid
2052   // For WebRTC clients: socket is INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE but transport is valid
2053   mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2054   bool has_socket = (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE);
2055   bool has_transport = (client->transport != NULL);
2056   mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2057 
2058   log_info("SEND_THREAD_VALIDATION: client_id=%s socket_valid=%d transport_valid=%d transport_ptr=%p",
2059            client->client_id, has_socket, has_transport, (void *)client->transport);
2060 
2061   if (!has_socket && !has_transport) {
2062     log_error("Invalid client connection in send thread (no socket or transport)");
2063     return NULL;
2064   }
2065 
2066   log_info("Started send thread for client %s (%s)", client->client_id, client->display_name);
2067 
2068   // Mark thread as running
2069   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, true);
2070 
2071   log_info("SEND_THREAD_LOOP_START: client_id=%s active=%d shutting_down=%d running=%d", client->client_id,
2072            atomic_load_bool(&client->active), atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down),
2073            atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running));
2074 
2075   // Track timing for video frame sends
2076   uint64_t last_video_send_time = 0;
2077   const uint64_t video_send_interval_us = 16666; // 60fps = ~16.67ms
2078 
2079   // High-frequency audio loop - separate from video frame loop
2080   // to ensure audio packets are sent immediately, not rate-limited by video
2081 #define MAX_AUDIO_BATCH 8
2082   int loop_iteration_count = 0;
2083   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) &&
2084          atomic_load_bool(&client->active) && atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running)) {
2085     loop_iteration_count++;
2086     bool sent_something = false;
2087     uint64_t loop_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2088     log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] START: client=%s", loop_iteration_count, client->client_id);
2089 
2090     // PRIORITY: Drain all queued audio packets before video
2091     // Audio must not be rate-limited by video frame sending (16.67ms)
2092     queued_packet_t *audio_packets[MAX_AUDIO_BATCH];
2093     int audio_packet_count = 0;
2094 
2095     if (client->audio_queue) {
2096       // Try to dequeue multiple audio packets
2097       for (int i = 0; i < MAX_AUDIO_BATCH; i++) {
2098         audio_packets[i] = packet_queue_try_dequeue(client->audio_queue);
2099         if (audio_packets[i]) {
2100           audio_packet_count++;
2101         } else {
2102           break; // No more packets available
2103         }
2104       }
2105       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2106         log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_AUDIO: client=%s dequeued=%d packets", client->client_id,
2107                       audio_packet_count);
2108       }
2109     } else {
2110       log_warn("Send thread: audio_queue is NULL for client %s", client->client_id);
2111     }
2112 
2113     // Send batched audio if we have packets
2114     if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2115       // Protect crypto field access with mutex
2116       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2117       bool crypto_ready = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
2118                           crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
2119       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2120 
2121       // Audio packets cannot be sent until crypto handshake completes (protocol requirement)
2122       if (!crypto_ready) {
2123         log_dev_every(1000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SKIP_AUDIO: client=%s handshake not ready, dropping %d audio packets",
2124                       client->client_id, audio_packet_count);
2125         // Drop audio packets until handshake completes
2126         for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2127           packet_queue_free_packet(audio_packets[i]);
2128         }
2129         audio_packet_count = 0;
2130       }
2131 
2132       asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
2133 
2134       // Only send audio if packets survived crypto check (count > 0 after potential drop)
2135       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2136         if (audio_packet_count == 1) {
2137           // Single packet - send directly for low latency using ACIP transport
2138           packet_type_t pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(audio_packets[0]->header.type);
2139 
2140           // Get transport reference while holding mutex briefly (prevents deadlock on TCP buffer full)
2141           mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2142           if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2143             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2144             log_warn("BREAK_AUDIO_SINGLE: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2145                      atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2146             break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2147           }
2148           acip_transport_t *transport = client->transport;
2149           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2150 
2151           // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2152           uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client->client_id);
2153           result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, pkt_type, audio_packets[0]->data, audio_packets[0]->data_len,
2154                                              client_id_hash);
2155           if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2156             log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL: client=%s, len=%zu, result=%d", client->client_id, audio_packets[0]->data_len,
2157                       result);
2158           }
2159         } else {
2160           // Multiple packets - batch them together and send via transport (works for all client types)
2161           packet_type_t first_pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(audio_packets[0]->header.type);
2162 
2163           // Get transport reference
2164           mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2165           if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2166             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2167             log_warn("BREAK_AUDIO_BATCH: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2168                      atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2169             result = ERROR_NETWORK;
2170           } else {
2171             acip_transport_t *transport = client->transport;
2172             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2173 
2174             if (first_pkt_type == PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH) {
2175               // Opus packets - batch and send via transport
2176               size_t total_opus_size = 0;
2177               for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2178                 total_opus_size += audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2179               }
2180 
2181               uint8_t *batched_opus = SAFE_MALLOC(total_opus_size, uint8_t *);
2182               uint16_t *frame_sizes = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)audio_packet_count * sizeof(uint16_t), uint16_t *);
2183 
2184               if (batched_opus && frame_sizes) {
2185                 size_t offset = 0;
2186                 for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2187                   frame_sizes[i] = (uint16_t)audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2188                   memcpy(batched_opus + offset, audio_packets[i]->data, audio_packets[i]->data_len);
2189                   offset += audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2190                 }
2191                 result = acip_send_audio_opus_batch(transport, batched_opus, total_opus_size, frame_sizes,
2192                                                     (uint32_t)audio_packet_count, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE, 20);
2193                 if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2194                   log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL (opus batch): client=%u, frames=%d, total_size=%zu, result=%d",
2195                             client->client_id, audio_packet_count, total_opus_size, result);
2196                 }
2197               } else {
2198                 log_error("Failed to allocate buffer for Opus batch");
2199                 result = ERROR_MEMORY;
2200               }
2201               SAFE_FREE(batched_opus);
2202               SAFE_FREE(frame_sizes);
2203             } else {
2204               // Raw float audio - batch and send via transport
2205               size_t total_samples = 0;
2206               for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2207                 total_samples += audio_packets[i]->data_len / sizeof(float);
2208               }
2209 
2210               float *batched_audio = SAFE_MALLOC(total_samples * sizeof(float), float *);
2211               if (batched_audio) {
2212                 size_t offset = 0;
2213                 for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2214                   size_t packet_samples = audio_packets[i]->data_len / sizeof(float);
2215                   memcpy(batched_audio + offset, audio_packets[i]->data, audio_packets[i]->data_len);
2216                   offset += packet_samples;
2217                 }
2218                 result = acip_send_audio_batch(transport, batched_audio, (uint32_t)total_samples, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
2219                 if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2220                   log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL (raw batch): client=%u, packets=%d, samples=%zu, result=%d",
2221                             client->client_id, audio_packet_count, total_samples, result);
2222                 }
2223               } else {
2224                 log_error("Failed to allocate buffer for audio batch");
2225                 result = ERROR_MEMORY;
2226               }
2227               SAFE_FREE(batched_audio);
2228             }
2229           }
2230         }
2231       } // End of if (audio_packet_count > 0)
2232 
2233       // Free all audio packets
2234       for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2235         packet_queue_free_packet(audio_packets[i]);
2236       }
2237 
2238       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2239         if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2240           log_error("Failed to send audio to client %s: %s", client->client_id, asciichat_error_string(result));
2241         }
2242         // Network errors corrupt the TCP stream - must disconnect immediately
2243         if (result == ERROR_NETWORK) {
2244           log_error("CLOSING_CLIENT_ON_NETWORK_ERROR: client_id=%s due to audio send failure (corrupted stream)",
2245                     client->client_id);
2246           break; // Exit send loop to trigger cleanup
2247         }
2248         log_warn("SKIP_AUDIO_ERROR: client_id=%s result=%d (continuing to send video)", client->client_id, result);
2249         // Continue sending video for non-network errors (audio is optional for browser clients)
2250       }
2251 
2252       sent_something = true;
2253       uint64_t audio_done_ns = time_get_ns();
2254       char audio_elapsed_str[32];
2255       time_pretty(audio_done_ns - loop_start_ns, -1, audio_elapsed_str, sizeof(audio_elapsed_str));
2256       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] AUDIO_SENT: took %s", loop_iteration_count,
2257                      audio_elapsed_str);
2258 
2259       // Small sleep to let more audio packets queue (helps batching efficiency)
2260       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2261         platform_sleep_us(100); // 0.1ms - minimal delay
2262       }
2263     } else {
2264       // No audio packets - brief sleep to avoid busy-looping, then check for other tasks
2265       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] NO_AUDIO: sleeping 1ms", loop_iteration_count);
2266       platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms - enough for audio render thread to queue more packets
2267 
2268       // Check if session rekeying should be triggered
2269       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2270       bool should_rekey = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
2271                           crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx) &&
2272                           crypto_handshake_should_rekey(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
2273       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2274 
2275       if (should_rekey) {
2276         log_debug("Rekey threshold reached for client %s, initiating session rekey", client->client_id);
2277         mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2278         // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on transport buffers
2279         mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2280         if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2281           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2282           mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2283           log_warn("BREAK_REKEY: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2284                    atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2285           break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2286         }
2287         acip_transport_t *rekey_transport = client->transport;
2288         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2289 
2290         // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the send_mutex (client_state_mutex still held for crypto state)
2291         asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_rekey_request(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, rekey_transport);
2292         mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2293 
2294         if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2295           log_error("Failed to send REKEY_REQUEST to client %s: %d", client->client_id, result);
2296         } else {
2297           log_debug("Sent REKEY_REQUEST to client %s", client->client_id);
2298           // Notify client that session rekeying has been initiated (old keys still active)
2299           log_info_client(client, "Session rekey initiated - rotating encryption keys");
2300         }
2301       }
2302     }
2303 
2304     // Always consume frames from the buffer to prevent accumulation
2305     // Rate-limit the actual sending, but always mark frames as consumed
2306     uint64_t video_check_ns = time_get_ns();
2307     if (!client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
2308       // Buffer has been destroyed (client is shutting down).
2309       // Exit cleanly instead of looping forever trying to access freed memory.
2310       log_warn("⚠️  Send thread exiting: outgoing_video_buffer is NULL for client %s (client shutting down?)",
2311                client->client_id);
2312       break;
2313     }
2314 
2315     // Get latest frame from double buffer (lock-free operation)
2316     // This marks the frame as consumed even if we don't send it yet
2317     const video_frame_t *frame = video_frame_get_latest(client->outgoing_video_buffer);
2318     uint64_t frame_get_ns = time_get_ns();
2319     char frame_get_elapsed_str[32];
2320     time_pretty(frame_get_ns - video_check_ns, -1, frame_get_elapsed_str, sizeof(frame_get_elapsed_str));
2321     log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] VIDEO_GET_FRAME: took %s, frame=%p", loop_iteration_count,
2322                    frame_get_elapsed_str, (void *)frame);
2323     log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Send thread: video_frame_get_latest returned %p for client %s", (void *)frame,
2324                   client->client_id);
2325 
2326     // Check if get_latest failed (buffer might have been destroyed)
2327     if (!frame) {
2328       log_warn("⚠️  Send thread exiting: video_frame_get_latest returned NULL for client %s (buffer destroyed?)",
2329                client->client_id);
2330       break; // Exit thread if buffer is invalid
2331     }
2332 
2333     // Check if it's time to send a video frame (60fps rate limiting)
2334     // Only rate-limit the SEND operation, not frame consumption
2335     uint64_t current_time_ns = time_get_ns();
2336     uint64_t current_time_us = time_ns_to_us(current_time_ns);
2337     uint64_t time_since_last_send_us = current_time_us - last_video_send_time;
2338     log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2339                   "Send thread timing check: time_since_last=%llu us, interval=%llu us, should_send=%d",
2340                   (unsigned long long)time_since_last_send_us, (unsigned long long)video_send_interval_us,
2341                   (time_since_last_send_us >= video_send_interval_us));
2342 
2343     if (current_time_us - last_video_send_time >= video_send_interval_us) {
2344       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✓ SEND_TIME_READY: client_id=%s time_since=%llu interval=%llu",
2345                      client->client_id, (unsigned long long)time_since_last_send_us,
2346                      (unsigned long long)video_send_interval_us);
2347       uint64_t frame_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2348 
2349       // GRID LAYOUT CHANGE: Check if render thread has buffered a frame with different source count
2350       // If so, send CLEAR_CONSOLE before sending the new frame
2351       int rendered_sources = atomic_load_bool(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources);
2352       int sent_sources = atomic_load_bool(&client->last_sent_grid_sources);
2353 
2354       if (rendered_sources != sent_sources && rendered_sources > 0) {
2355         // Grid layout changed! Send CLEAR_CONSOLE before next frame using ACIP transport
2356         // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2357         mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2358         if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2359           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2360           log_warn("BREAK_CLEAR_CONSOLE: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2361                    atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2362           break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2363         }
2364         acip_transport_t *clear_transport = client->transport;
2365         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2366 
2367         // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2368         acip_send_clear_console(clear_transport);
2369         log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_FAST, "Client %s: Sent CLEAR_CONSOLE (grid changed %d → %d sources)",
2370                         client->client_id, sent_sources, rendered_sources);
2371         atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, rendered_sources);
2372         sent_something = true;
2373       }
2374 
2375       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2376                      "🎯 FRAME_VALIDATION: client_id=%s frame=%p, frame->data=%p, frame->size=%zu", client->client_id,
2377                      (void *)frame, (void *)frame->data, frame->size);
2378 
2379       if (!frame->data) {
2380         log_info("❌ SKIP_NO_DATA: client_id=%s frame=%p data=%p", client->client_id, (void *)frame,
2381                  (void *)frame->data);
2382         continue;
2383       }
2384       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ FRAME_DATA_OK: client_id=%s data=%p", client->client_id,
2385                      (void *)frame->data);
2386 
2387       if (frame->data && frame->size == 0) {
2388         log_info("❌ SKIP_ZERO_SIZE: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame->size);
2389         platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms sleep
2390         continue;
2391       }
2392       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ FRAME_SIZE_OK: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame->size);
2393 
2394       // Snapshot frame metadata (safe with double-buffer system)
2395       const char *frame_data = (const char *)frame->data; // Pointer snapshot - data is stable in front buffer
2396       size_t frame_size = frame->size;                    // Size snapshot - prevent race condition with render thread
2397       uint32_t width = client->width;
2398       uint32_t height = client->height;
2399       uint64_t step1_ns = time_get_ns();
2400       uint64_t step2_ns = time_get_ns();
2401       uint64_t step3_ns = time_get_ns();
2402       uint64_t step4_ns = time_get_ns();
2403 
2404       // Check if crypto handshake is complete before sending (prevents sending to unauthenticated clients)
2405       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2406       bool crypto_ready = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) ||
2407                           (client->crypto_initialized && crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
2408       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2409 
2410       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2411                      "🔐 CRYPTO_CHECK: client_id=%s crypto_ready=%d crypto_initialized=%d no_encrypt=%d",
2412                      client->client_id, crypto_ready, client->crypto_initialized, GET_OPTION(no_encrypt));
2413 
2414       if (!crypto_ready) {
2415         log_info("⚠️  SKIP_SEND_CRYPTO: client_id=%s crypto_initialized=%d no_encrypt=%d", client->client_id,
2416                  client->crypto_initialized, GET_OPTION(no_encrypt));
2417         continue; // Skip this frame, will try again on next loop iteration
2418       }
2419       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ CRYPTO_READY: client_id=%s about to send frame", client->client_id);
2420 
2421       // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2422       // ACIP transport handles header building, CRC32, encryption internally
2423       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2424                     "Send thread: About to send frame to client %s (width=%u, height=%u, size=%zu, data=%p)",
2425                     client->client_id, width, height, frame_size, (void *)frame_data);
2426 
2427       // Log first 32 bytes of frame data to verify we can access it
2428       if (frame_data && frame_size > 0) {
2429         log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2430                        "FRAME_DATA_HEX: client=%u first_bytes=[%02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x "
2431                        "%02x %02x %02x %02x %02x]",
2432                        client->client_id, ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[0], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[1],
2433                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[2], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[3], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[4],
2434                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[5], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[6], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[7],
2435                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[8], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[9], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[10],
2436                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[11], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[12], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[13],
2437                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[14], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[15]);
2438       }
2439 
2440       mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2441       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2442         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2443         log_warn("BREAK_FRAME_SEND: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p loop_iter=%d", client->client_id,
2444                  atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport, loop_iteration_count);
2445         break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2446       }
2447       acip_transport_t *frame_transport = client->transport;
2448       mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2449 
2450       // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2451       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_ASCII_FRAME: client_id=%s size=%zu width=%u height=%u",
2452                     client->client_id, frame_size, width, height);
2453       uint64_t send_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2454       log_dev("[SEND_LOOP_%d] FRAME_SEND_START: size=%zu", loop_iteration_count, frame_size);
2455       asciichat_error_t send_result =
2456           acip_send_ascii_frame(frame_transport, frame_data, frame_size, width, height, client->client_id);
2457       uint64_t send_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2458       char send_elapsed_str[32];
2459       time_pretty(send_end_ns - send_start_ns, -1, send_elapsed_str, sizeof(send_elapsed_str));
2460       log_dev("[SEND_LOOP_%d] FRAME_SEND_END: took %s, result=%d", loop_iteration_count, send_elapsed_str, send_result);
2461       uint64_t step5_ns = time_get_ns();
2462 
2463       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2464         if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2465           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send video frame to client %s: %s", client->client_id,
2466                     asciichat_error_string(send_result));
2467         }
2468         log_error("SEND_FRAME_FAILED: client_id=%s result=%d message=%s loop_iter=%d", client->client_id, send_result,
2469                   asciichat_error_string(send_result), loop_iteration_count);
2470         log_warn("BREAK_SEND_ERROR: client_id=%s (frame send failed)", client->client_id);
2471         break;
2472       }
2473 
2474       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_FRAME_SUCCESS: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame_size);
2475 
2476       // Increment frame counter and log
2477       unsigned long frame_count = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&client->frames_sent_count, 1) + 1;
2478       log_info("🎬 FRAME_SENT: client_id=%s frame_num=%lu size=%zu", client->client_id, frame_count, frame_size);
2479 
2480       sent_something = true;
2481       last_video_send_time = current_time_us;
2482 
2483       uint64_t frame_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2484       uint64_t frame_time_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(frame_start_ns, frame_end_ns));
2485       if (frame_time_us > 15 * US_PER_MS_INT) { // Log if sending a frame takes > 15ms (encryption adds ~5-6ms)
2486         uint64_t step1_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(frame_start_ns, step1_ns));
2487         uint64_t step2_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step1_ns, step2_ns));
2488         uint64_t step3_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step2_ns, step3_ns));
2489         uint64_t step4_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step3_ns, step4_ns));
2490         uint64_t step5_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step4_ns, step5_ns));
2491         log_warn_every(
2492             LOG_RATE_DEFAULT,
2493             "SEND_THREAD: Frame send took %.2fms for client %s | Snapshot: %.2fms | Memcpy: %.2fms | CRC32: %.2fms | "
2494             "Header: %.2fms | send_packet_secure: %.2fms",
2495             frame_time_us / 1000.0, client->client_id, step1_us / 1000.0, step2_us / 1000.0, step3_us / 1000.0,
2496             step4_us / 1000.0, step5_us / 1000.0);
2497       }
2498     }
2499 
2500     // If we didn't send anything, sleep briefly to prevent busy waiting
2501     if (!sent_something) {
2502       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] IDLE_SLEEP: nothing sent", loop_iteration_count);
2503       platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms sleep
2504     }
2505     uint64_t loop_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2506     uint64_t loop_elapsed_ns = loop_end_ns - loop_start_ns;
2507     if (loop_elapsed_ns > 10 * NS_PER_MS) {
2508       char loop_elapsed_str[32];
2509       time_pretty(loop_elapsed_ns, -1, loop_elapsed_str, sizeof(loop_elapsed_str));
2510       log_warn("[SEND_LOOP_%d] SLOW_ITERATION: took %s (client=%u)", loop_iteration_count, loop_elapsed_str,
2511                client->client_id);
2512     }
2513   }
2514 
2515   // Log why the send thread exited
2516   log_warn("Send thread exit conditions - client_id=%s g_should_exit=%d shutting_down=%d active=%d "
2517            "send_thread_running=%d",
2518            client->client_id, atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit), atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down),
2519            atomic_load_bool(&client->active), atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running));
2520 
2521   // Mark thread as stopped
2522   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
2523   log_debug("Send thread for client %s terminated", client->client_id);
2524 
2525   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
2526   asciichat_errno_destroy();
2527 
2528   return NULL;
2529 }






References acip_send_ascii_frame(),
    acip_send_audio_batch(), acip_send_audio_opus_batch(),
    acip_send_clear_console(), client_info::active,
    asciichat_errno_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_fetch_add_u64(), atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), atomic_store_u64(),
    client_info::audio_queue, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE,
    client_info::client_id, client_info::client_state_mutex,
    client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), crypto_handshake_rekey_request(),
    crypto_handshake_should_rekey(),
    client_info::crypto_initialized, queued_packet_t::data_len,
    client_info::display_name, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    frame, client_info::frames_sent_count, g_should_exit,
    GET_OPTION, client_info::height, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    client_info::last_rendered_grid_sources,
    client_info::last_sent_grid_sources, log_debug,
    log_debug_every, log_dev, log_dev_every,
    log_error, log_info, log_info_client,
    log_info_every, LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, LOG_RATE_FAST,
    log_warn, log_warn_every, MAX_AUDIO_BATCH,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    NS_PER_MS, client_info::outgoing_video_buffer,
    packet_queue_free_packet(), packet_queue_try_dequeue(),

    platform_sleep_us(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    client_info::send_mutex, client_info::send_thread_running,
    SET_ERRNO, client_info::shutting_down,
    client_info::socket, time_elapsed_ns(), time_get_ns(),
    time_pretty(), client_info::transport, US_PER_MS_INT,
    video_frame_get_latest(), and client_info::width.






client_info_t * find_client_by_id (const char *
  client_id)


Fast O(1) client lookup by ID using hash table. This is the
    primary method for locating clients throughout the server. It uses a hash
    table for constant-time lookups regardless of client count, making it
    suitable for high-performance operations like rendering and stats.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(1) average case, O(n) worst case (hash collision)
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Hash table is internally thread-safe for lookups
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Called by render threads to find target clients for frame generation
    • 
  
  • Used by protocol handlers to locate clients for packet processing
    • 
  
  • Stats collection for per-client performance monitoring
    • 



Parameters


client_id Unique identifier for the client (0 is
  invalid)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found or invalid ID


Note


Does not require external locking - hash table provides
  thread safety



Returns direct pointer to client struct - caller should use
    snapshot pattern




Definition at line 392 of file
  src/server/client.c.


392                                                         {
393   if (!client_id || client_id[0] == '\0') {
394     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid client ID");
395     return NULL;
396   }
397 
398   // Protect uthash lookup with read lock to prevent concurrent access issues
399   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
400 
401   client_info_t *result = NULL;
402   HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, result);
403 
404   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
405 
406   if (!result) {
407     log_warn("Client not found for ID %s", client_id);
408   }
409 
410   return result;
411 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, g_client_manager,
    g_client_manager_rwlock, log_warn, rwlock_rdlock,
    rwlock_rdunlock, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(),
    crypto_server_cleanup_client(),
    crypto_server_decrypt_packet(),
    crypto_server_encrypt_packet(), crypto_server_get_context(),
    crypto_server_is_ready(), and
  start_webrtc_client_threads().






client_info_t * find_client_by_socket (socket_t
  socket)


Find client by socket descriptor using linear search. This
    function provides socket-based client lookup, primarily used during
    connection establishment before client IDs are assigned. Less efficient than
    find_client_by_id() but necessary for socket-based operations.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(n) where n = number of active clients
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Internally acquires read lock on
    g_client_manager_rwlock
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Connection establishment during add_client() processing
    • 
  
  • Socket error handling and cleanup operations
    • 
  
  • Debugging and diagnostic functions
    • 



Parameters


socket Platform-abstracted socket descriptor to
  search for


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found


Note


Only searches active clients (avoids returning stale
  entries)



Caller should use snapshot pattern when accessing returned client
    data




Definition at line 436 of file
  src/server/client.c.


436                                                       {
437   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
438 
439   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
440     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].socket == socket && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
441       client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
442       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
443       return client;
444     }
445   }
446 
447   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
448   return NULL;
449 }

Process a decrypted packet from a client




Parameters


client Client info structure


type Packet type


data Packet data


len Packet length

  src/server/client.c.

3757   if (type == 5000) { // CLIENT_CAPABILITIES
3758     log_debug("CLIENT: client_id=%s, data=%p, len=%zu", client->client_id, data, len);
3759   }
3760 
3761   // Rate limiting: Check and record packet-specific rate limits
3762   if (g_rate_limiter) {
3763     if (!check_and_record_packet_rate_limit(g_rate_limiter, client->client_ip, client->socket, type)) {
3764       // Rate limit exceeded - error response already sent by utility function
3765       return;
3766     }
3767   }
3768 
3769   // O(1) dispatch via hash table lookup
3770   int idx = client_dispatch_hash_lookup(g_client_dispatch_hash, type);
3771   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3772     log_error("DISPATCH_LOOKUP: type=%d, idx=%d (len=%zu)", type, idx, len);
3773   }
3774   if (idx < 0) {
3775     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Unknown packet type: %d (len=%zu)", type, len);
3776     return;
3777   }
3778 
3779   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3780     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, calling handler[%d]...", type, idx);
3781   }
3782   g_client_dispatch_handlers[idx](client, data, len);
3783   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3784     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, handler returned", type);
3785   }
3786 }

int
  process_encrypted_packet (client_info_t * client, packet_type_t
  * type, void ** data, size_t * len, uint32_t * sender_id)


Process an encrypted packet from a client




Parameters


client Client info structure


type Pointer to packet type (will be updated with decrypted type)


data Pointer to packet data (will be updated with decrypted data)


len Pointer to packet length (will be updated with decrypted length)


sender_id Pointer to sender ID (will be updated with decrypted sender
  ID)


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error

  src/server/client.c.


2815                                                   {
2816   if (!crypto_server_is_ready(client->client_id)) {
2817     log_error("Received encrypted packet but crypto not ready for client %s", client->client_id);
2818     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2819     *data = NULL;
2820     return -1;
2821   }
2822 
2823   // Store original allocation size before it gets modified
2824   size_t original_alloc_size = *len;
2825   void *decrypted_data = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, original_alloc_size);
2826   size_t decrypted_len;
2827   int decrypt_result = crypto_server_decrypt_packet(client->client_id, (const uint8_t *)*data, *len,
2828                                                     (uint8_t *)decrypted_data, original_alloc_size, &decrypted_len);
2829 
2830   if (decrypt_result != 0) {
2831     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to process encrypted packet from client %s (result=%d)", client->client_id,
2832               decrypt_result);
2833     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, original_alloc_size);
2834     buffer_pool_free(NULL, decrypted_data, original_alloc_size);
2835     *data = NULL;
2836     return -1;
2837   }
2838 
2839   // Replace encrypted data with decrypted data
2840   // Use original allocation size for freeing the encrypted buffer
2841   buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, original_alloc_size);
2842 
2843   *data = decrypted_data;
2844   *len = decrypted_len;
2845 
2846   // Now process the decrypted packet by parsing its header
2847   if (*len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
2848     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Decrypted packet too small for header from client %s", client->client_id);
2849     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2850     *data = NULL;
2851     return -1;
2852   }
2853 
2854   packet_header_t *header = (packet_header_t *)*data;
2855   *type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
2856   *sender_id = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->client_id);
2857 
2858   // Adjust data pointer to skip header
2859   *data = (uint8_t *)*data + sizeof(packet_header_t);
2860   *len -= sizeof(packet_header_t);
2861 
2862   return 0;
2863 }

int
  remove_client (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char *
  client_id)


Definition at line 1332 of file
  src/server/client.c.

1335     return -1;
1336   }
1337

1339   client_info_t *target_client = NULL;
1340   char display_name_copy[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
1341   socket_t client_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1342 
1343   log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Attempting to remove client %s", client_id);

1345 
1346   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1347     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
1348     if (strcmp(client->client_id, client_id) == 0 && client->client_id[0] != '\0') {

1351       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1352         rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1353         log_debug("Client %s already being removed by another thread, skipping", client_id);
1354         return 0; // Return success - removal is in progress
1355       }
1356       // Mark as shutting down and inactive immediately to stop new operations
1357       log_debug("Setting active=false in remove_client (client_id=%s, socket=%d)", client_id, client->socket);
1358       log_info("Removing client %s (socket=%d) - marking inactive and clearing video flags", client_id, client->socket);
1359       atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, true);
1360       atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
1361       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, false);
1362       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, false);
1363       target_client = client;
1364 
1365       // Store display name before clearing
1366       SAFE_STRNCPY(display_name_copy, client->display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN - 1);
1367 
1368       // Save socket for tcp_server_stop_client_threads() before closing
1369       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1370       client_socket = client->socket; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1371       // NOTE: Do NOT call socket_shutdown() here - it sends FIN to peer and breaks the connection
1372       // The threads will exit naturally when they check shutting_down/active flags or hit errors
1373       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1374 
1375       // Shutdown packet queues to unblock send thread
1376       if (client->audio_queue) {
1377         packet_queue_stop(client->audio_queue);
1378       }
1379       // Video now uses double buffer, no queue to shutdown
1380 
1381       break;
1382     }
1383   }
1384 
1385   // If client not found, unlock and return
1386   if (!target_client) {
1387     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1388     log_warn("Cannot remove client %s: not found", client_id);
1389     return -1;
1390   }
1391 
1392   // Unregister client from session_host (for discovery mode support)
1393   // NOTE: Client may not be registered if crypto handshake failed before session_host registration
1394   if (server_ctx->session_host && target_client->session_client_id != 0) {
1395     asciichat_error_t session_result =
1396         session_host_remove_client(server_ctx->session_host, target_client->session_client_id);
1397     if (session_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1398       // ERROR_NOT_FOUND (91) is expected if client failed crypto before being registered with session_host
1399       if (session_result == ERROR_NOT_FOUND) {
1400         log_debug("Client %s not found in session_host (likely failed crypto before registration)", client_id);
1401       } else {
1402         log_warn("Failed to unregister client %s from session_host: %s", client_id,
1403                  asciichat_error_string(session_result));
1404       }
1405     } else {
1406       log_debug("Client %s unregistered from session_host", client_id);
1407     }
1408   }
1409 
1410   // Release write lock before joining threads.
1411   // This prevents deadlock with render threads that need read locks.
1412   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1413 
1414   // Phase 2: Stop all client threads
1415   // For TCP clients: use tcp_server thread pool management
1416   // For WebRTC clients: manually join threads (no socket-based thread pool)
1417   // Use is_tcp_client flag, not socket value - socket may already be INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE
1418   // even for TCP clients if it was closed earlier during cleanup.
1419   log_debug("Stopping all threads for client %s (socket %d, is_tcp=%d)", client_id, client_socket,
1420             target_client ? target_client->is_tcp_client : -1);
1421 
1422   if (target_client && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1423     // TCP client: use tcp_server thread pool
1424     // This joins threads in stop_id order: receive(1), render(2), send(3)
1425     // Use saved client_socket for lookup (tcp_server needs original socket as key)
1426     if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1427       asciichat_error_t stop_result = tcp_server_stop_client_threads(server_ctx->tcp_server, client_socket);
1428       if (stop_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1429         log_warn("Failed to stop threads for TCP client %s: error %d", client_id, stop_result);
1430         // Continue with cleanup even if thread stopping failed
1431       }
1432     } else {
1433       log_debug("TCP client %s socket already closed, threads should have already exited", client_id);
1434     }
1435   } else if (target_client) {
1436     // WebRTC client: manually join threads
1437     log_debug("Stopping WebRTC client %s threads (receive and send)", client_id);
1438 
1439     // Join receive thread (but skip if called from the receive thread itself to avoid deadlock)
1440     thread_id_t current_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1441     if (asciichat_thread_equal(current_thread_id, target_client->receive_thread_id)) {
1442       log_debug("remove_client() called from receive thread for client %s, skipping self-join", client_id);
1443     } else {
1444       void *recv_result = NULL;
1445       asciichat_error_t recv_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->receive_thread, &recv_result);
1446       if (recv_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1447         log_warn("Failed to join receive thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, recv_join_result);
1448       } else {
1449         log_debug("Joined receive thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1450       }
1451     }
1452 
1453     // Join dispatch thread (BEFORE destroying packet queues)
1454     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->dispatch_thread)) {
1455       atomic_store_bool(&target_client->dispatch_thread_running, false);
1456       void *dispatch_result = NULL;
1457       asciichat_error_t dispatch_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->dispatch_thread, &dispatch_result);
1458       if (dispatch_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1459         log_warn("Failed to join dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, dispatch_join_result);
1460       } else {
1461         log_debug("Joined dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1462       }
1463     }
1464 
1465     // Join send thread
1466     void *send_result = NULL;
1467     asciichat_error_t send_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->send_thread, &send_result);
1468     if (send_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1469       log_warn("Failed to join send thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, send_join_result);
1470     } else {
1471       log_debug("Joined send thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1472     }
1473     // Note: Render threads still need to be stopped - they're created the same way for both TCP and WebRTC
1474     // For now, render threads are expected to exit when they check g_should_exit and client->active
1475   }
1476 
1477   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
1478   // For WebSocket clients: LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already closed and destroyed the transport
1479   // Trying to destroy it again causes heap-use-after-free since LWS callbacks might still fire
1480   // For TCP clients: transport is ours to clean up
1481   if (target_client && target_client->transport && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1482     acip_transport_destroy(target_client->transport);
1483     target_client->transport = NULL;
1484     log_debug("Destroyed ACIP transport for TCP client %s", client_id);
1485   } else if (target_client && target_client->transport && !target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1486     // WebSocket client - just NULL it out, LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already destroyed it
1487     target_client->transport = NULL;
1488     log_debug("Skipped transport destruction for WebSocket client %s (LWS already destroyed)", client_id);
1489   }
1490 
1491   // Now safe to close the socket (threads are stopped)
1492   if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1493     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Closing socket %d for client %s after thread cleanup", client_socket, client_id);
1494     socket_close(client_socket);
1495   }
1496 
1497   // Phase 3: Clean up resources with write lock
1498   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1499 
1500   // Re-validate target_client pointer after reacquiring lock.
1501   // Another thread might have invalidated the pointer while we had the lock released.
1502   if (target_client) {
1503     // Verify client_id still matches and client is still in shutting_down state
1504     bool still_shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&target_client->shutting_down);
1505     if (strcmp(target_client->client_id, client_id) != 0 || !still_shutting_down) {
1506       log_warn("Client %s pointer invalidated during thread cleanup (id=%s, shutting_down=%d)", client_id,
1507                target_client->client_id, still_shutting_down);
1508       rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1509       return 0; // Another thread completed the cleanup
1510     }
1511   }
1512 
1513   // Mark socket as closed in client structure
1514   if (target_client && target_client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1515     mutex_lock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1516     target_client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
1517     mutex_unlock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1518     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Client %s socket set to INVALID", target_client->client_id);
1519   }
1520 
1521   // Use the dedicated cleanup function to ensure all resources are freed
1522   cleanup_client_all_buffers(target_client);
1523 
1524   // Remove from audio mixer
1525   if (g_audio_mixer) {
1526     mixer_remove_source(g_audio_mixer, client_id);
1527 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1528     log_debug("Removed client %s from audio mixer", client_id);
1529 #endif
1530   }
1531 
1532   // Remove from uthash table
1533   // Verify client is actually in the hash table before deleting.
1534   // Another thread might have already removed it.
1535   if (target_client) {
1536     client_info_t *hash_entry = NULL;
1537     HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client->client_id, hash_entry);
1538     if (hash_entry == target_client) {
1539       HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client);
1540       log_debug("Removed client %s from uthash table", client_id);
1541     } else {
1542       log_warn("Client %s already removed from hash table by another thread (found=%p, expected=%p)", client_id,
1543                (void *)hash_entry, (void *)target_client);
1544     }
1545   } else {
1546     log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from hash table (client not found)", client_id);
1547   }
1548 
1549   // Cleanup crypto context for this client
1550   if (target_client->crypto_initialized) {
1551     crypto_handshake_destroy(&target_client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
1552     target_client->crypto_initialized = false;
1553     log_debug("Crypto context cleaned up for client %s", client_id);
1554   }
1555 
1556   // Verify all threads have actually exited before resetting client_id.
1557   // Threads that are still starting (at RtlUserThreadStart) haven't checked client_id yet.
1558   // We must ensure threads are fully joined before zeroing the client struct.
1559   // Use exponential backoff for thread termination verification
1560   int retry_count = 0;
1561   const int max_retries = 5;
1562   while (retry_count < max_retries && (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->send_thread) ||
1563                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->receive_thread) ||
1564                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->video_render_thread) ||
1565                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->audio_render_thread))) {
1566     // Exponential backoff: 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms
1567     uint32_t delay_ms = 10 * (1 << retry_count);
1568     log_warn("Client %s: Some threads still appear initialized (attempt %d/%d), waiting %ums", client_id,
1569              retry_count + 1, max_retries, delay_ms);
1570     platform_sleep_us(delay_ms * 1000);
1571     retry_count++;
1572   }
1573 
1574   if (retry_count == max_retries) {
1575     log_error("Client %s: Threads did not terminate after %d retries, proceeding with cleanup anyway", client_id,
1576               max_retries);
1577   }
1578 
1579   // Only reset client_id to 0 AFTER confirming threads are joined
1580   // This prevents threads that are starting from accessing a zeroed client struct
1581   // Reset client_id to NULL before destroying mutexes to prevent race conditions.
1582   // This ensures worker threads can detect shutdown and exit before the mutex is destroyed.
1583   // If we destroy the mutex first, threads might try to access a destroyed mutex.
1584   target_client->client_id[0] = '\0'; // Clear the client_id string
1585 
1586   // Wait for threads to observe the client_id reset
1587   // Use sufficient delay for memory visibility across all CPU cores
1588   platform_sleep_us(5 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 5ms delay for memory barrier propagation
1589 
1590   // Destroy mutexes and condition variables
1591   // IMPORTANT: Always destroy these even if threads didn't join properly
1592   // to prevent issues when the slot is reused
1593   mutex_destroy(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1594   mutex_destroy(&target_client->send_mutex);
1595   cond_destroy(&target_client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1596 
1597   // Clear client structure
1598   // NOTE: After memset, the mutex handles are zeroed but the OS resources
1599   // have been released by the destroy calls above
1600   memset(target_client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1601 
1602   // Recalculate client count
1603   int remaining_count = 0;
1604   for (int j = 0; j < MAX_CLIENTS; j++) {
1605     if (g_client_manager.clients[j].client_id[0] != '\0') {
1606       remaining_count++;
1607     }
1608   }
1609   g_client_manager.client_count = remaining_count;
1610 
1611   log_debug("Client removed: client_id=%s (%s) removed, remaining clients: %d", client_id, display_name_copy,
1612             remaining_count);
1613 
1614   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1615 
1616   // Broadcast updated state
1617   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1618 
1619   return 0;
1620 }

int
  start_webrtc_client_threads (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char
  * client_id)


Start threads for a WebRTC client after crypto initialization.
    This is called by WebSocket handler after crypto handshake is initialized.
    It ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto
    context exists.




Parameters


server_ctx Server context


client_id Client ID to start threads for


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

2679     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Server context is NULL");
2680     return -1;
2681   }
2682 
2683   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
2684   if (!client) {
2685     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client %s not found", client_id);
2686     return -1;
2687   }
2688 
2689   log_debug("Starting threads for WebRTC client %s...", client_id);
2690   return start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false);
2691 }

void
  stop_client_threads (client_info_t * client)


Definition at line 2693 of file

2694   if (!client) {
2695     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client is NULL");
2696     return;
2697   }
2698 
2699   // Signal threads to stop
2700   log_debug("Setting active=false in stop_client_threads (client_id=%s)", client->client_id);

Variable
  Documentation




client_manager_t g_client_manager


Global client manager singleton - central coordination point.
    Global client manager.




This is the primary data structure for managing all connected
    clients. It serves as the bridge between main.c's connection accept loop and
    the per-client threading architecture.




STRUCTURE COMPONENTS:


    
  
  • clients[]: Array backing storage for client_info_t structs
    • 
  
  • client_hashtable: O(1) lookup table for client_id ->
    client_info_t*
    • 
  
  • client_count: Current number of active clients
    • 
  
  • mutex: Legacy mutex (mostly replaced by rwlock)
    • 
  
  • next_client_id: Monotonic counter for unique client identification
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: Protected by g_client_manager_rwlock for concurrent
    access


Definition at line 336 of file
  src/server/client.c.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    any_clients_sending_video(),
    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), find_client_by_id(),
    find_client_by_socket(), remove_client(),
    send_server_state_to_client(), server_main(),
    stats_logger_thread(), and update_server_stats().






rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock = {0}


Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager.
    Read-write lock protecting client manager.




This lock enables high-performance concurrent access patterns:


    
  
  • Multiple threads can read client data simultaneously (stats,
    rendering)
    • 
  
  • Only one thread can modify client data at a time (add/remove
    operations)
    • 
  
  • Eliminates contention between read-heavy operations
    • 



USAGE PATTERN:


    
  
  • Read operations: rwlock_rdlock() for client lookups, stats
      gathering
    • 
  
  • Write operations: rwlock_wrlock() for add_client(),
      remove_client()
    • 
  
  • Always acquire THIS lock before per-client mutexes (lock ordering)
    • 



Definition at line 351 of file
  src/server/client.c.


351 {0};

    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), find_client_by_id(),
    find_client_by_socket(), remove_client(),
    server_main(), stats_logger_thread(), and
    update_server_stats().
